description_long: This is a classic red corded telephone featuring a rectangular plastic base with rounded edges and a glossy finish. The handset is also red, ergonomically shaped, and connected to the base by a long, black coiled cord. The base houses a prominent rotary dial with white numbering and a beige dial plate, as well as a small rectangular window likely for a label or number card. Four small feet are visible at the corners of the base, elevating it slightly above the surface. The cradle for the handset is integrated into the top of the base, with visible slots for the earpiece and mouthpiece to rest securely. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-to-late 20th-century telephones commonly found in homes and offices.
description: A vintage red corded telephone with a rotary dial, glossy plastic base, and a coiled cord connecting the handset. The handset rests on a built-in cradle atop the base.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the rotary dial, cradle, and handset fully separated from the base with the coiled cord extended.
description_view_1: Angled front-left view highlighting the rotary dial, the cradle, and the handset lying beside the base with the cord partially coiled.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the length of the coiled cord and the profile of the base, with the handset placed further from the base.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back of the handset and the base, with the coiled cord extending outward and the rotary dial partially visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
